feed.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage

entry.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
entry.summary:
The recent discussions around the SegData design have highlighted some misunderstandings that need to be addressed to clarify the proposal's intent and structure. Firstly, it is crucial to emphasize that within the retention window, every node is required to enforce all rules, which aligns with the existing parameters such as block size and transaction weight. This foundational aspect ensures that despite any misconceptions, the design adheres to established blockchain protocols.
Furthermore, there appears to be confusion regarding the concept of free carriage within the SegData framework. The proposal clearly outlines that the system operates under specific regulations which must be upheld by all nodes during the retention period. This mechanism is integral to maintaining the integrity and functionality of the network.
Currently, the proposal remains active and open for further discussion. It is essential for all stakeholders to engage in this dialogue to reach a comprehensive understanding and address any ambiguities that have arisen. This ongoing conversation will hopefully lead to refinements that enhance the proposal's clarity and effectiveness in practical applications.
